bibdata.title: The International System of Units
Recommended values of standard frequencies for applications including the practical realization of the metre and secondary representations of the second

Iodine (λ≈612⁢nm
)
Le système international d’unités
Valeurs recommandées des fréquences étalons destinées à la mise en pratique de la définition du mètre et aux représentations secondaires de la seconde

Iodine (λ≈612⁢nm
)

organization.name: Bureau International des Poids et Mesures
Bureau International des Poids et Mesures
Bureau International des Poids et Mesures

localized-strings.localized-string: Terms, definitions, symbols and abbreviated terms

localized-string.p: No terms and definitions are listed in this document.
For the purposes of this document, the following terms and definitions apply.

localized-strings.localized-string: The following referenced documents are indispensable for the application of this document. For dated references, only the edition cited applies. For undated references, the latest edition of the referenced document (including any amendments) applies.
There are no normative references in this document.

localized-string.p: For the purposes of this document, the terms and definitions given in % apply.
For the purposes of this document, the terms and definitions given in % and the following apply.

clause.p: © Bureau International des Poids et Mesures 2003 all rights reserved

clause.title: Copyright statement
clause.p: This document is distributed under the terms of the Creative Commons Attribution 4.0 International License (), which permits unrestricted use, distribution, and reproduction in any medium, provided you give appropriate credit to the original author(s) and the source, provide a link to the Creative Commons license, and indicate if changes were made.

feedback-statement.p:
BIPM
Pavillon de Breteuil
F-92312 Sèvres Cedex
FRANCE

p.strong: Absorbing molecule 127I2, a7 component, R(47) 9-2 transition

fn.p: All transitions in I2 refer to the B3Π 0u+−X1 Σg+ system.

clause.title: 1.CIPM recommended values
clause.p:
with a relative standard uncertainty of 3×10−10 apply to the radiation of a He-Ne laser stabilized with an iodine cell, within or external to the laser, having a cold-finger temperature of −5±2⁢°C
For the specification of operating conditions, such as temperature, modulation width and laser power, the symbols ± refer to a tolerance, not an uncertainty..

clause.title: 2.Source data
clause.p: Other available values having relative uncertainties higher than 3.0×10−10 have not been used. The relative standard uncertainty calculated from the dispersion of the six values is 2.8×10−10, which the CCL preferred to round up to 3.0×10−10.

Using the recommended value of the absorbing molecule 127I2, a16 or f component, R(127) 11-5 transition (see iodine at λ≈633⁢nm
and frequency differences listed in corresponding Table 1) one obtains

Using the recommended value of the absorbing molecule 127I2, a16 or f component, R(127) 11-5 transition (see iodine at λ≈633⁢nm
and frequency differences listed in corresponding Table 1) one obtains

Using the recommended value of the absorbing molecule 127I2, a16 or f component, R(127) 11-5 transition (see iodine at λ≈633⁢nm
and frequency differences listed in corresponding Table 1) one obtains

From the measured value (see Table 4 below)
fb15−fa7=−160 318⁢kHz
uc=3⁢kHz

Using the recommended value of the absorbing molecule 127I2, a16 or f component, R(127) 11-5 transition (see iodine at λ≈633⁢nm
and frequency differences listed in corresponding Table 1) one obtains

This value is a result of the frequency ratio fa13/fe, to which the recommended value adopted by the CIPM in 1983 [6], [7] was applied, i.e. fi=473 612 214.8⁢MHz
. (see iodine at λ≈633⁢nm
and frequency differences listed in corresponding Table 1)

Using the recommended value of the absorbing molecule 127I2, a16 or f component, R(127) 11-5 transition (see iodine at λ≈633⁢nm
and frequency differences listed in corresponding Table 1) one obtains

Knowing the frequency difference (see Table 1)
fa7−fa13=−249 600⁢kHz
uc=10⁢kHz
,

clause.title: 3.Absolute frequency of the other transitions related to those adopted as recommended and frequency intervals between transitions and hyperfine components
clause.p: These tables replace those published in BIPM Com. Cons. Long., 2001, 10, 184-187 and Metrologia, 2003, 40, 127-128.
The notation for the transitions and the components is that used in the source references. The values adopted for the frequency intervals are the weighted means of the values given in the references.
For the uncertainties, account has been taken of:

li.p: the uncertainties given by the authors;
the spread in the different determinations of a single component;
the effect of any perturbing components;
the difference between the calculated and the measured values.

clause.p: In the tables, uc represents the estimated combined standard uncertainty (1σ ).
All transitions in molecular iodine refer to the B-X system.

bibitem.formattedref: CCDM/82-34, NPL, Laser wavelength Measurements, May 1982.
CCDM/82-19a, BIPM, Réponse au questionnaire CCDM/82-3.

Bönsch G., Gläser M., Spieweck F., Bestimmung der Wellenlängenverhältnisse von drei 127I2-stabilisierten Lasern bei 515⁢nm
, 612⁢nm
und 633⁢nm
, PTB Jahresbericht, 1986, 161.

Vitushkin L. F., Zakharenko Yu. G., Yvanov I. V., Leibengardt G. I., Shur V. L., Measurements of Wavelength of High-Stabilized He-Ne/I2 Laser at 612⁢nm
, Opt. Spectr., 1990, 68, 705-707.

Himbert M., Bouchareine P., Hachour A., Juncar P., Millerioux Y., Razet A., Measurements of Optical Wavelength Ratios Using a Compensated Field Sigmameter, IEEE Trans. Instrum. Meas., 1991, 40, 200-203.
BIPM, Proc. Verb. Com. Int. Poids et Mesures, 1983, 51.
Documents Concerning the New Definition of the Metre, Metrologia, 1984, 19, 163-178.
Recommendation CCL3 (BIPM Com. Cons. Long., 10th Meeting, 2001) adopted by the Comité International des Poids et Mesures at its 91th Meeting as Recommendation 1 (CI-2002).
Gläser M., Hyperfine Components of Iodine for Optical Frequency Standards PTB-Bericht, 1987, PTB-Opt-25.

Razet A., Millerioux Y., Juncar P., Hyperfine Structure of the 47R(9-2), 48P(11-3) and 48R(15-5) Lines of 127I2 at 612⁢nm
as Secondary Standards of Optical Frequency, Metrologia, 1991, 28, 309-316.

Cérez P., Bennett S. J., Helium-neon laser stabilized by saturated absorption in iodine at 612⁢nm
, Appl. Opt.,1979, 18, 1079-1083.

Gläser M., Properties of a He-Ne Laser at λ≈612⁢nm
, Stabilized by Means of an External Iodine Absorption Cell, IEEE Trans. Instrum. Meas., 1987, IM-36, 604-608.
Bertinetto F., Cordiale P., Fontana S., Picotto G. B., Recent Progresses in He-Ne Lasers Stabilized to 127I2, IEEE Trans. Instrum. Meas., 1985, IM-34, 256-261.

Robertsson L., Iodine-stabilized He-Ne lasers at λ=612⁢nm
using internal and external cells, BIPM Proc.-Verb. Com. Int. Poids et Mesures, 1992, 60, 160-162.

Bertinetto F., Cordiale P., Picotto G. B., Chartier J.-M., Felder R., Gläser M., Comparison Between the 127I2 Stabilized He-Ne Lasers at 633⁢nm
and at 612⁢nm
of the BIPM and the IMGC, IEEE Trans. Instrum. Meas., 1983, IM-32, 72-76.

Kegung D., Gläser M., Helmcke J., I2 Stabilized He-Ne Lasers at 612⁢nm
, IEEE Trans. Instrum. Meas., 1980, IM-29, 354-357.

Ciddor P. E., Brown N., Hyperfine Spectra in Iodine-129 at 612⁢nm
, Opt. Commun., 1980, 34, 53-56.

Gläser M., Kegung D., Foth H. J., Hyperfine Structure and Fluorescence Analysis of Enriched 129I2 at the 612⁢nm
Wavelength of the He-Ne Laser, Opt. Commun., 1981, 38, 119-123.
